| The White House • February 6, 2019
---
| | |
# **‘I ask you to choose greatness’**

**Last night, President Donald J. Trump** previewed the next chapter of his presidency.

**“The agenda I will lay** out this evening is not a Republican agenda or a Democrat agenda,” the President told Congress as he opened his second State of the Union address. “It’s the agenda of the American people.” What unites most of our citizens isn’t political party—it’s complete awe and frustration at Washington’s decades of dysfunction.

**Americans watching at home** agreed. Three-quarters of them approved of the President’s speech, according to a CBS News poll conducted last night. Even more revealing, a full _82 percent_ of independents endorsed President Trump’s message.

**That makes the choice clear** for our leaders in Congress and across government: Voters will reward results over resistance. Compromise can rebuild trust in a broken system. Pointless destruction and gridlock won’t—and both come with a steep political price.

**Choosing greatness** is a vision that can break decades of political stalemate. It’s an agenda that all Americans, Republican or Democrat, can get behind:

1. A [booming economy](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=d75d954ffe&e=3ee4985eea) can continue to lift up all working people.

2. Congress should champion a [safe, legal immigration system.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e8002f1603&e=3ee4985eea)

3. We can invest in [real infrastructure](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=179edb904a&e=3ee4985eea) that leaves no community behind.

4. [New trade deals](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=397cfe7703&e=3ee4985eea) will ensure that no country exploits our workers.

5. Washington must rein in the [outrageous price of prescription drugs.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6319bc4477&e=3ee4985eea)

6. America will [rebuild its military](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=5d0180ad76&e=3ee4985eea) while ending the cycle of endless wars.

7. We must [support working parents](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=01c441f1e1&e=3ee4985eea) and protect the gift of life.

8. From criminal justice reform to opioids, let’s [embrace bipartisan success.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=1976455eed&e=3ee4985eea)

9. Both parties can be guided by a [shared vision of American greatness.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=83d870fbdf&e=3ee4985eea)

[President Trump delivers his second State of the Union Address](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=aebf3530ee&e=3ee4985eea).

**More** : [See what Republicans AND Democrats are saying about the speech.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=cb116ed3a6&e=3ee4985eea)

# **Video of the day: First Lady welcomes special guests**

**Some serve in law enforcement.** Others survived drug addiction, criminal violence, or cancer. Some only needed a second chance to prove they could turn their lives around.

**The First Lady** and President Trump’s special guests for last night’s State of the Union all have unique stories to tell. Together, they tell the story of America.

**Watch:** [First Lady Melania Trump welcomes the 2019 State of the Union guests](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=e6f3948308&e=3ee4985eea)

name: Steve Chabot
subject: My Thoughts on the State of the Union Last Night
body:
My Thoughts on the State of the Union Last Night No Images? [Click here](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-e-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-jy/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-r/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-y/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-j/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-t/)
### [My Thoughts on the State of the Union Last Night](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-i/)
"Madam Speaker, the President of the United States!" loudly exclaimed the Sergeant at Arms last night as President Trump entered the U.S. House of Representatives. I attended last night, as I have for 22 prior State of the Union Addresses. From Bill Clinton to George W. Bush to Barack Obama to Donald Trump. They're part of an American tradition that goes back to George Washington giving the first Address to Congress at Federal Hall in New York City back in 1790 (the city of Washington didn't exist yet.) For about 100 years following President Thomas Jefferson, our presidents just sent a written speech over, until Woodrow Wilson gave his in person once again, and the rest of our presidents have followed Wilson's example. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-d/) _Previous Weekly Posts_ [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-h/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-k/)
### [Are We Headed for Another Shutdown?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-u/)
Well the longest government shutdown in U.S. history is finally over. Of course we could be right back in the middle of another one in less than three weeks. So what's going to happen? [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-o/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-b/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-n/)
### [What the heck is a "Green New Deal"?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-p/)
Well, the partial government shutdown drags on. Today is day 33 - the longest shutdown in U.S. history. We've seen Speaker Pelosi uninvite President Trump to the State of the Union. We've seen President Trump counterpunch, and cancel Pelosi's plane ride to the Middle East. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-x/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-m/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-c/)
### [Hold on to Your Wallet](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-q/)
There are a whole bunch of Democrats who would like to be "the chosen one" to run against President Trump in the next election. Elizabeth Warren of Massachusetts, Tulsi Gabbard of Hawaii, and Julian Castro of Texas are a few who have already thrown their hats into the ring. I would expect a slew of others to jump in in the upcoming weeks and months. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-a/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-f/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-z/)
### [Is "A Wall an Immorality"?](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-v/)
Last week, Nancy Pelosi, who is once again the most powerful Member of the U.S. House of Representatives, claimed that "a wall is an immorality." Is she right? In my opinion, absolutely not. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-e/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-s/) [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-g/)
### [My Predictions for 2019](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-w/)
Well, it's that time of year again. Time for me to go out on a limb, and predict what's going to happen in the upcoming year. Here goes. [Read this entry >>](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-yd/) [Donate](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-yh/) [Get a Yard Sign](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-yk/) | [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-yu/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-jl/)| [](https://start.rightmailer.net/t/r-l-jtddljx-kujkulhluu-jr/)

name: Jess Fassler

body:
Peter,
I spent last weekend on the road with Kirsten in New Hampshire, and I can affirmatively say: **this is fun**. Kirsten is a great campaigner and **[enjoying every second of it](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a7741/1064748425/VEsH/ "https://twitter.com/meredithk27/status/1091823918602694656")** --and it shows!
But before we get into the rest of the update, I wanted to make sure you saw **[this article](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a7740/1064748425/VEsE/ "https://talkingpointsmemo.com/dc/kirsten-gillibrand-james-zadroga-september-11")** about how Kirsten helped pass the 9/11 health bill. When she believes in something, she fights for it with everything she's got - and this article is a **must read**.
On Friday, Kirsten **[brought down the house](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a7743/1064748425/VEsF/ "https://www.wmur.com/article/gillibrand-kicks-off-weekend-of-campaigning-in-nh/26118057")** with an overflow crowd at happy hour with New Hampshire Young Dems. (Check out **[this great Boston Globe clip](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a7742/1064748425/VEsC/ "https://www.bostonglobe.com/metro/2019/02/02/senator-kirsten-gillibrand-reaches-out-voters-with-populist-message/JQuyFzHtMhW0JTknQkCd4O/story.html")**.) Then she roused us up early to hit a **[6:30 a.m. spin class](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a774d/1064748425/VEsD/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1091695328854794240")** before heading out for **[coffee with local Democrats](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a774c/1064748425/VEsA/ "http://www.nashuatelegraph.com/news/local-news/2019/02/02/gillibrand-draws-full-house-to-coffeehouse/")** in Nashua and then to a **[main street tour in downtown Portsmouth](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a774f/1064748425/VEsB/ "https://www.seacoastonline.com/news/20190202/sen-gillibrand-tours-downtown-portsmouth")**. THEN on our way to meet with UNH students on campus, we had an **[impromptu stop for sledding](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a774e/1064748425/VEsO/ "https://www.facebook.com/KirstenGillibrand/videos/1374207056052665/?q=kirsten%20gillibrand&epa=SEARCH_BOX")**. (Would love to see Trump try that.) Kirsten closed out the weekend heading to the North Country for a **[meeting with seniors](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a7749/1064748425/VEsP/ "https://www.caledonianrecord.com/news/local/presidential-campaigning-in-littleton-kirsten-gillibrand-first-candidate-to-visit/image_96704722-fe5b-532b-a014-b6f258f93032.html")** before returning to Washington for votes.
Tonight, she's taping an episode of Crooked Media's **Lovett or Leave It** for a live audience. Be sure to check out the episode when it drops.
_**In Other News:**_
- Glamour has a smart write-up on **[7 things Kirsten would champion as president](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a7748/1064748425/VEsHBQ/ "https://www.glamour.com/story/kirsten-gillibrand-issues")**.
- Sunday marked one year out from the Iowa caucus, and **[Kirsten announced she will be returning to the state on Presidents Day](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a774b/1064748425/VEsHBA/ "https://www.desmoinesregister.com/story/news/elections/presidential/caucus/2019/02/03/kirsten-gillibrand-iowa-visit-trip-stop-cedar-rapids-2020-caucus-presidential-candidate-february-19/2761822002/")**.
- Kirsten took to Twitter to dunk on Mitch McConnell for calling making Election Day a national holiday a **["power grab."](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a774a/1064748425/VEsHBw/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1090698060433223680")**
- Sen. Cory Booker joined the Democratic primary race, and Kirsten welcomed her friend with **[style and class](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b5/1064748425/VEsHBg/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1091334372689612800"). [Picking up on a theme here?](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b4/1064748425/VEsHAQ/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1087444716214337541")**
- Focusing on her day job, Kirsten **[kept up the pressure](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b7/1064748425/VEsHAA/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1091084815330209792")** on the Department of Defense after another year of horrible results in the attempt to stem the tide of sexual abuse at military service academies.
- Kirsten also **[sent a letter to the acting attorney general](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b6/1064748425/VEsHAw/ "https://twitter.com/ShaneGoldmacher/status/1092197103386939392")** on the **[detention complex in Brooklyn that has been without heat and power](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b1/1064748425/VEsHAg/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1091766169525116933")**.
- Finally, another great piece of content to **[share with your friends](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b0/1064748425/VEsHDQ/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1090999276090605568")**.
**Looking Forward:** Kirsten is off to South Carolina this weekend. Keep an eye out for more of her campaign updates from the trail!
That's it for now. As always, thanks for all you do.
Jess
P.S. No corporate PACs, no federal lobbyists and no individual super PAC. Just YOU. **[Give today](https://secure.kirstengillibrand.com/page/m/4f9769cd/2ae112ef/1705dc8f/357a77b3/1064748425/VEsHDA/ "https://twitter.com/SenGillibrand/status/1091155053967233025")**.

Friend,

Even before the State of the Union Address, Cryin' Chuck and Democrats criticized President Trump's speech.

During his historic State of the Union Address he had a very clear message for Democrats like Cryin' Chuck, who only obstruct:
_" Victory is not winning for our party, victory is winning for our _**country.**_ "_

name: Greg Stanton
subject: making progress on gun safety
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Stanton for Congress
body:
For too long, people in Congress have ignored the vast majority of Americans who want action on gun control.

_That changes this year. _

**I am proud to support H.R. 8, a bill that will require universal background checks on nearly all firearm sales. **

[](https://act.myngp.com/el/SHM3d-_OJVjlK9KAzExDPvADHYMw6FBT7uEIqX6PDPA=/_0vpH9TiPBLj2WCA9vHdqN0GuswfNbDCi23afR8ZZjE=)

We know that the gun lobby is not going to like this and are going to put up a fight to defeat this bill.

[**Will you help us show that we have the people on our side by becoming a citizen co-sponsor of H.R. 8? **](https://act.myngp.com/el/SHM3d-_OJVjlK9KAzExDPvADHYMw6FBT7uEIqX6PDPA=/_0vpH9TiPBLj2WCA9vHdqN0GuswfNbDCi23afR8ZZjE=)

There is more work to be done but this is a necessary step in the right direction.

Thank you for your support of this critical bill,

_\-- Greg _

subject: Ivanka Trump: Empower Women to Foster Freedom
body:
“President Trump will sign a memorandum establishing the Women’s Global Development and Prosperity Initiative, or W-GDP” this afternoon. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=b78913d029&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| | |
# **Ivanka Trump: Empower Women to Foster Freedom**

“President Trump will sign a memorandum establishing the Women’s Global Development and Prosperity Initiative, or W-GDP” this afternoon, Advisor to the President Ivanka Trump writes in _The Wall Street Journal_.

“This initiative aims to help 50 million women in developing countries realize their economic potential by 2025 . . . Expanding women’s economic participation has the potential to boost global economic output by an additional $12 trillion by 2025.” W-GDP has three core pillars: advancing workforce development and vocational training; empowering women as entrepreneurs; and eliminating the legal, regulatory, and cultural barriers that prevent women from participating in their local economies.

[Click here to read more.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=abfb4d6699&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
| “Leading up to Tuesday night, we heard about the president’s intention to unify a country seldom so divided. So we expected a lot of easy talk about holding hands and trying to get along. This was more than words, at some points becoming an event that included women in House Speaker Nancy Pelosi’s caucus cheering the president as if they supported him,” _[The Gazette](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=02d7af470c&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. Their conclusion: Tuesday night’s State of the Union address “probably ranks high among history’s best.”
---
|
---
| “Ivanka Trump is unveiling an effort aimed at helping 50 million women in the developing world get ahead economically over the next six years,” Catherine Lucey reports for [_The Associated Press_.](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=dcfe49935c&e=3ee4985eea) “Ivanka Trump, who will attend the Munich Security Conference next week to promote [W-GDP], stressed that she sees this as a national security priority. ‘We think women are arguably the most under-tapped resource in the developing world for accelerating economic growth and prosperity,’ she told _The Associated Press_."
---
|
---
| David Malpass is a great choice from President Trump for the tough job of leading the World Bank, _[The Wall Street Journal](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=9795f68751&e=3ee4985eea)_ editorial board writes. “He is an evangelist for pro-growth policies including low taxes, spending control, stable money for the poor as much as for the rich, and the rule of law. This is controversial in some corners of the World Bank, where they measure success not by growth but by how much money gets shoveled out the door.”
---
|

name: Michael Lighty, The Sanders Institute
subject: Medicare For All - How Do We Pay For It?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE SANDERS INSTITUTE
body:
# [](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5c5/bed43eb/9ea04ab9/75fbe1b2/4174662742/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=email-header")
Dear Friend,
Who knew 2019 would already feature a major national debate over Medicare for All?
The Sanders Institute.
We anticipated growing public support for Medicare for All...and also the pushback from skeptics. How many of you have talked with family or friends about it and not been met with the response, "Sounds great, but how do we pay for it?"
At The Sanders Institute Gathering in December, Sanders Institute Fellow Bob Pollin and I answered that question and released an in-depth study, _"Economic Analysis of the Medicare for All Act of 2017"_ done by a team of researchers at the Political Economy Research Institute (PERI) led by Bob.
**[You can watch the video of our discussion here:](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5c5/bed43eb/9ea04ab9/75fbe1bd/4174662742/VEsE/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/medicare-for-all_-i-like-it-how-do-we-pay-for-it")**
[](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5c5/bed43eb/9ea04ab9/75fbe1bd/4174662742/VEsF/ "https://www.sandersinstitute.com/blog/medicare-for-all_-i-like-it-how-do-we-pay-for-it")
The independent study answers the question of how to pay for Medicare for All in great detail. Our current, ridiculously expensive system absorbs 18% of U.S. GDP and leaves nearly 30 million people uninsured and another 40 million underinsured with large deductibles and copays.
It's time for a change.
The study shows how, under Medicare for All, every single resident of the U.S. can be **guaranteed good-quality health care at an overall cost that is nearly 10% less than our current system** -- savings that can translate into **lower****health-care costs for both households and businesses. Average businesses that cover their workers will lower their costs by 8%. Middle-class households who now buy insurance on their****own****will see a savings of 14% of their income.** That is, their net income will rise by 14% through Medicare for All.
On top of these cost savings, none of us will ever again be uninsured or have to fight with an insurance company for coverage or worry about having to cover co-pays or deductibles while living on a tight budget, or live in fear of somebody in our family getting sick and facing bankruptcy as a result.
The study also considers in depth how to provide a Just Transition to people now employed in the private health insurance industry--providing them with retraining, relocation, job placement, as well as direct financial support as the country moves away from our existing private health insurance system.
Just to emphasize: **none of this is 'pie-in-the-sky.' Rather, the study reflects the reality of every other similar economy in the world.** They guarantee healthcare for all with no barriers to care. This saves lives and costs less.
The study was peer-reviewed by a group of eminent experts on the subject. One of the reviewers, Professor Jeffrey Sachs of Columbia University, concluded that, "This study is the most comprehensive, detailed, authoritative study ever undertaken of Medicare for All, and it points powerfully and unassailably in support of MFA."
Bob and I had the honor of presenting this research again at The Democratic Senators Issues Conference last week.
**Bringing this kind of truth to the policy debate is what makes the Sanders Institute unique - rigorous research, innovative and common sense policy, presented in dialogue with a range of advocates to a variety of audiences in order to engage all of us in the issues that really matter.**
Please join us in supporting the on-going work of this extraordinary resource - The Sanders Institute, committed to transforming our country for the benefit of all.
Michael Lighty
Sanders Institute Fellow
[You can view the full study and peer reviews here.](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5c5/bed43eb/9ea04ab9/75fbe1bc/4174662742/VEsC/ "https://www.peri.umass.edu/")
**[DONATE»](https://sandersinstitute-ourrev.bsd.net/page/m/2ba7a5c5/bed43eb/9ea04ab9/75fbe1bf/4174662742/VEsD/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/sandersinstitute?refcode=em190207")**

name: Sherrod Brown
subject: Trump says another shutdown "certainly an option"
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown
body:
Sign the petition and help us fight
|
---

| | | | Trump says another shutdown "certainly an option" \-- _Vanity Fair_ ; 1/2019
---
During last month's government shutdown, thousands of Ohio workers missed two paychecks. Hundreds were forced to file for jobless benefits. Nationwide, around 800,000 federal workers were furloughed or went to work for no pay. They couldn't pay their rent, childcare bills, or credit card bills. Ohio businesses were hurt as well. Many lost business. Some had to cancel plans for expansion and growth.
**Yet President Trump says throwing Americans' lives into chaos once again is "certainly an option" if he doesn't get the funding he wants for his ridiculous border wall.**
Putting extreme campaign promises ahead of Americans' well-being is no way to govern.
[**If you agree, say so. Sign the petition to demand Trump drop his threat to shut down the government again.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc07/4462dc26/8f6f2c84/201ed256/1063533059/VEsH/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190207m")
Border security is important. But let's be clear here: Building a wall will not solve our country's immigration problems. The issue is far more nuanced than that.
We should set aside money for real, effective border security efforts and use the rest to solve problems all across the country -- like fixing our crumbling infrastructure.
This government cannot effectively serve the American people if we're hopping from one shutdown to the next. Federal workers, contractors, and their families do not deserve to have their lives and livelihoods upended every time the President makes a demand.
[**Let Trump know you won't stand for another shutdown.**](https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/page/m/302ffc07/4462dc26/8f6f2c84/201ed256/1063533059/VEsE/ "https://secure.sherrodbrown.com/e190207m")
With gratitude,
Sherrod

clean_body: Sign the petition and help us fight | | | | | Trump says another shutdown "certainly an option" \-- Vanity Fair ; 1/2019 During last month's government shutdown, thousands of Ohio workers missed two paychecks. Hundreds were forced to file for jobless benefits. Nationwide, around 800,000 federal workers were furloughed or went to work for no pay. They couldn't pay their rent, childcare bills, or credit card bills. Ohio businesses were hurt as well. Many lost business. Some had to cancel plans for expansion and growth. Yet President Trump says throwing Americans' lives into chaos once again is "certainly an option" if he doesn't get the funding he wants for his ridiculous border wall. Putting extreme campaign promises ahead of Americans' well-being is no way to govern. "" Border security is important. But let's be clear here: Building a wall will not solve our country's immigration problems. The issue is far more nuanced than that. We should set aside money for real, effective border security efforts and use the rest to solve problems all across the country -- like fixing our crumbling infrastructure. This government cannot effectively serve the American people if we're hopping from one shutdown to the next. Federal workers, contractors, and their families do not deserve to have their lives and livelihoods upended every time the President makes a demand. "" With gratitude, Sherrod | "" | | Paid for by Friends of Sherrod Brown Contributions or gifts to Friends of Sherrod Brown are not tax deductible.

name: Kamala Harris
subject: Green New Deal
body:
I wanted to share some really important news with you this morning: today,
I’m proud to announce that I’ve signed on as an original co-sponsor of the
Green New Deal in the Senate.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/Green-New-Deal?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190207&source=ema190207&t=1001&akid=2732%2E5755789%2E8kAepY ]Add your name right now to stand with me in support of the Green New
Deal resolution to combat climate change and jump-start our economy.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/Green-New-Deal?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190207&source=ema190207&t=1002&akid=2732%2E5755789%2E8kAepY ]Add my name

For too long, we have been governed by lawmakers who are beholden to big
oil and big coal. They have refused to act on climate change. So it’s on
us to speak the truth, rooted in science fact, not science fiction.

Here’s the truth: climate change is real, and it is an existential threat
to our country, our planet, and our future. With each passing day, the
imminent threat of climate change grows -- and we see it in everything
from more instances of extreme weather to rapidly melting glaciers.

According to a harrowing report from the U.N. Intergovernmental Panel on
Climate Change, we have a shrinking window to take drastic action to cut
carbon emissions and make meaningful change to save our planet.

The Green New Deal is a bold plan to drastically shift our country to 100%
clean and renewable energy -- all while guaranteeing jobs to American
workers. We will repair our country’s crumbling infrastructure, upgrade
buildings across the nation, and dramatically cut greenhouse gas
emissions.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/Green-New-Deal?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190207&source=ema190207&t=1003&akid=2732%2E5755789%2E8kAepY ]We don’t have much time to make the urgent changes we need to protect
ourselves and our planet. Add your name right now to stand with me in
support of the Green New Deal.

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/Green-New-Deal?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190207&source=ema190207&t=1004&akid=2732%2E5755789%2E8kAepY ]Add my name

Bold action takes bold leadership, and I’m grateful to Representative
Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez and Senator Ed Markey for leading the charge on
this critical resolution.

We do not fight this fight for our generation alone -- but for generations
to come. Thank you for taking direct action today.

Sincerely,

— Kamala Harris

[ https://action.kamalaharris.org/sign/Green-New-Deal?firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&addr1=&city=Cresco&state=PA&zip=18326&phone=None&employer=&occupation=&refcode=ema190207&source=ema190207&t=1005&akid=2732%2E5755789%2E8kAepY ]Add my name

You can unsubscribe from this mailing list at any time:
http://action.kamalaharris.org/cms/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?akid=2732%2E5755789%2E8kAepY

name: Tina Smith
subject: Inside look: how Women Winning helped me win
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
They are already recruiting, training, and mentoring pro-choice women who will be on the ballot in 2020.

| Friend, At the time I was appointed to the U.S. Senate, I had only ten months to launch and run a winning campaign. Though I had been a public servant for years, I had never run for office before. The task ahead of me was great, but I was not alone. **A non-profit called _Women Winning_ immediately sprang into action. _Women Winning_ was the first organization to endorse my campaign, host a fundraiser (and went on to host four more, raising tens of thousands of dollars), and help build my team -- they even opened my campaign P.O. Box!** This support was not unique to my campaign -- for more than 37 years, _Women Winning_ has worked determinedly to elect pro-choice women to all levels of office in Minnesota, and their work is incredibly effective. Minnesota now leads the nation as 1 of only 5 states with two pro-choice women U.S. Senators. In fact, 50% of Minnesota's federal delegation is comprised of pro-choice women! The way that _Women Winning_ gets behind their candidates is unparalleled, and they are already recruiting, training, and mentoring pro-choice women who will be on the ballot in 2020. **[Please help me and dozens of other pro-choice women candidates WIN by making a donation to _Women Winning_ today.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d270/c1f7015/61b3ee11/1140fe87/3663507874/VEsH/ "https://secure.everyaction.com/LL7s3ENA3EqaQZLpzY3TcA2")** [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d270/c1f7015/61b3ee11/1140fe87/3663507874/VEsE/ "https://secure.everyaction.com/LL7s3ENA3EqaQZLpzY3TcA2") Hearing directly from you is the best way to ensure I'm fighting for everything you care about in the Senate. Thank you for supporting me and all of the pro-choice women running in 2020. Tina P.S. Your contribution will help _Women Winning_ build for the crucial 2020 election. **[Chip in today to help them elect candidates like me.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4d270/c1f7015/61b3ee11/1140fe87/3663507874/VEsF/ "https://secure.everyaction.com/LL7s3ENA3EqaQZLpzY3TcA2")**

name: Jay Inslee
subject: They're starting to listen
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
You've heard me say this before, and I won't stop now.

| [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a0f9/2f5ea88d/7933c852/2cbde5ba/886545794/VEsH/ "https://www.facebook.com/6227578465/posts/10157059650423466/") Friend -- Today is a momentous day for our climate movement. A Green New Deal resolution has been officially introduced in Congress by Sen. Markey, my former colleague, and Rep. Ocasio-Cortez. Now, leaders on both sides of the aisle in the other Washington need to hear our united call for action. You've heard me say it before, but I won't stop -- confronting climate change must be our national mission. This is our opportunity to create millions of new jobs. It's our shot to lead the world in building a more just, inclusive, and innovative clean energy economy. I am thrilled that the leaders behind the Green New Deal are demanding that Congress respond to the greatest threat of our generation. [**Let's show Sen. Markey and Rep. Ocasio-Cortez that we have their backs! Share my message of support right now on Facebook.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a0f9/2f5ea88d/7933c852/2cbde5ba/886545794/VEsE/ "https://www.facebook.com/6227578465/posts/10157059650423466/") [](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a0f9/2f5ea88d/7933c852/2cbde5ba/886545794/VEsF/ "https://www.facebook.com/6227578465/posts/10157059650423466/") The Green New Deal has forced climate action back onto Congress' agenda. Now this is a critical moment for the climate movement. We can choose to either let climate change destroy our way of life, or we can do what scientists have been telling us we must for years: Take real, immediate action. [**Please, share this message about the Green New Deal resolution on Facebook and show why it's never been more necessary for Congress to take serious climate action.**](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a0f9/2f5ea88d/7933c852/2cbde5ba/886545794/VEsC/ "https://www.facebook.com/6227578465/posts/10157059650423466/") Today is the start of something big -- let's get to work. Very truly yours, Jay Inslee
| [SHARE NOW »](https://action.jayinslee.com/page/m/7bd7a0f9/2f5ea88d/7933c852/2cbde5ba/886545794/VEsD/ "https://www.facebook.com/6227578465/posts/10157059650423466/")
